_meta.description: Test questions for Building Act 1993 (Vic) RAG prototype. Each question has the section(s) that contain the correct answer, based on content we've verified exists in the parsed chunks for Parts 3, 4, and 5.
_meta.corpus_scope: Building Act 1993 (Vic), Parts 3-5 only. Questions requiring Regulations, NCC, or other Parts are explicitly marked 'out_of_scope': true.

difficulty_codes.easy: Direct keyword match, one section answers it
difficulty_codes.medium: Requires reading multiple subsections or understanding structure
difficulty_codes.hard: Cross-references, exceptions, or interpretive judgment needed

_meta.how_to_use: Run each question through your retrieval system. Check whether the top-K results include any of the expected_sections. Track hit@1, hit@3, hit@5 as your quality metric. A good prototype should hit@3 >= 80% on easy questions before you worry about medium/hard.

questions.q: What is the penalty for carrying out building work without a building permit?
expected_facts: 500 penalty units (natural person)
2500 penalty units (body corporate)
16B is the indictable version with imprisonment up to 5 years
questions.notes: Section 16 is the standard offence; 16B is the aggravated/indictable version. A good answer should distinguish them.

questions.q: As an owner of land, am I responsible for making sure a building permit exists before work starts?
expected_facts: Owner must ensure a permit is in force
Exception if owner engaged a building practitioner/engineer/architect

questions.q: If a builder is named on a permit, what are their compliance obligations?
expected_facts: Must ensure work is carried out in accordance with the Act, Regulations, permit, and any binding determination
Penalty 500/2500 units

questions.q: If my permit gets suspended, is it a defence if I didn't know it had been suspended?
expected_facts: Yes if unaware and couldn't reasonably have known
Not available to architects or engaged building practitioners

questions.q: Who do I apply to for a building permit?
expected_facts: Application goes to a building surveyor (municipal or private)
Surveyor must give the owner an information statement

questions.q: Who can apply for an owner-builder certificate of consent?
expected_facts: A natural person may apply to the Authority
For domestic building work on land

questions.q: Is there a dollar threshold above which I must have an owner-builder certificate of consent?
expected_facts: Yes, if cost exceeds the prescribed amount
Penalty 120 penalty units if done without consent

questions.q: Can a building surveyor refuse to issue a permit?
expected_facts: Yes, on specified grounds
Further limitations apply to certain permits

questions.q: What happens if I need to change the builder named on an existing permit?
expected_facts: Must notify the relevant building surveyor
Surveyor may change the named builder subject to requirements in s24A/24B

questions.q: If I'm refused a building permit, am I entitled to reasons?
expected_facts: Yes, surveyor must notify applicant in writing without delay and give reasons

questions.q: Do councils keep a register of building permits?
expected_facts: Yes, councils must keep a register
Must be available for inspection during office hours
Records kept for prescribed period

questions.q: What are the mandatory notification stages during building work?
expected_facts: Builder must notify the relevant building surveyor at each mandatory notification stage
Inspection occurs at those stages
Penalty 120/600 units

questions.q: Who is allowed to perform building inspections?
expected_facts: Persons carrying out inspections must be registered

questions.q: If a building surveyor directs me to fix non-compliant work, what can the direction require?
expected_facts: Carry out work so building work complies with Act/Regs/permit
Stop further work that would prevent inspection

questions.q: Can I appeal or delay a direction to fix building work?
expected_facts: Extension of time available
Operation of direction may be stayed
Direction may be revoked

questions.q: What happens at the end of building work — what certificate do I need?
expected_facts: Certificate of final inspection

questions.q: Can I move into a new building before I get an occupancy permit?
expected_facts: No, if the building permit states an occupancy permit is required
Applies to whole building OR part of a building
Occupation must be in accordance with the permit
Penalty 120/600 units

questions.q: Who do I apply to for an occupancy permit?
expected_facts: Application to municipal building surveyor or private building surveyor
For places of public entertainment, some applications go to the Authority

questions.q: What is a building manual and when is one required?
expected_facts: Must provide draft building manual with occupancy permit application
Manual must be approved
Owner and owners corporation have update obligations

questions.q: What are the penalties for providing false information in an occupancy permit application?
expected_facts: It is an offence to include false or misleading information

questions.q: When can an occupancy permit be cancelled?
expected_facts: Cancellation for fraud
Cancellation on other grounds

questions.q: Can an occupancy permit be amended after it's issued?
expected_facts: Yes, amendment of permit or approval is provided for

questions.q: Is temporary occupation of a building allowed before the permanent occupancy permit?
expected_facts: Yes, temporary occupation can be approved
Specific people may apply
Determination process for applications

questions.q: For a place of public entertainment, what do I need before running a public event?
expected_facts: Entertainment must not be conducted without appropriate permit
Place must not be used for public entertainment without compliance
Use must be in accordance with occupancy permit

questions.q: Who gets notified when a building permit is issued on a property in a Development Victoria project area?
expected_facts: Notice to Development Victoria of application
Council to notify Development Victoria of issue of permit

questions.q: What's the process for demolition permits and when can they be suspended?
expected_facts: Specific application process for demolition permits
Suspension of demolition permit pending is provided for

questions.q: If I'm refused an owner-builder certificate of consent, can I appeal?
expected_facts: Review by VCAT

questions.q: How long is an owner-builder certificate of consent valid for, and what does it allow?
expected_facts: Certificate of consent issued under this section
questions.notes: This is the kind of question where the prototype will need to synthesize — the exact duration may be prescribed by regulation not Act.

questions.q: Does an occupancy permit apply to heritage or historic buildings?
expected_facts: Historic buildings and special buildings have specific provisions

questions.q: Can the Authority refuse to issue a building permit number?
expected_facts: Authority may refuse on specified grounds
Must notify applicant of refusal
Review by VCAT available

questions.q: Do I need a permit to build a pergola under 10 square metres?
expected_facts: Answer lives in Building Regulations 2018 Schedule 3 (exempt building work), not the Act
questions.notes: Included to test whether the system correctly says 'this is answered in the Regulations, not the Act' rather than hallucinating an Act section.

questions.q: What's the maximum stair riser height in a residential building?
expected_facts: Answer is NCC Volume 2 Part 3.9.1, not the Act
questions.notes: Out-of-scope test — should route to NCC, not invent an answer from the Act.
